# Project Overview — 3d-portal

**Document type:** Brownfield system documentation (generated by `bmad-document-project`, 2026-05-15)
**Audience:** Downstream BMAD planning workflows (product-brief, PRD, architecture, epics). Also: AI agents (Claude Code / Codex) reading this as primary context.
**Output language:** English (per `_bmad/bmm/config.yaml`).

---

## What this is

**3d-portal** is a self-hosted web portal for browsing and managing Michał's personal 3D-printing model collection. It lives in his homelab (single physical server, two LXC/Docker hosts on the LAN), is reachable from outside the LAN at `https://3d.ezop.ddns.net` via DDNS, and gates household access through nginx basic-auth at the edge plus JWT authentication for the admin surface.

The product replaces a previous one-way Windows → server `rsync` of an STL/photo folder tree with a **portal-owned source of truth** (SoT) that the household consumes through a polished web UI and that **AI agents** consume through a JSON HTTP surface plus a self-documenting `/agent-runbook`.

It is **not** a marketplace, not multi-tenant, not for sharing models to the public internet — it is a single-user-household catalog tool with a deliberate AI-agent secondary surface.

---

## Who this serves

**Primary user — Michał (Ezop), the owner-operator.**

- Solo DevOps engineer (banking IT), homelab hobbyist, 3D-printing enthusiast.
- Browses his own collection from desktop or phone, occasionally curates metadata (tags, categories, notes, prints log, external links).
- Triggers thumbnail renders when adding new models. Reviews audit log when something seems off.
- Generates share links to send a single model (STL + photos + renders) to a friend/family member outside the household.

**Secondary user — household members (read-only catalog browsers).**

- Reach the portal through household basic-auth on the edge nginx.
- Browse the same catalog Michał sees, but without admin actions (no model edit, no render trigger, no audit log).
- Currently no per-user accounts inside the portal — household auth is collective.

**Tertiary user — recipients of share links (outside the household).**

- Receive a URL like `/share/<token>` — bypasses basic-auth via nginx location rule, no JWT needed.
- View a single model's STL + photos + renders for as long as the share token TTL allows (TTL stored in Redis).

**Quaternary user (deliberately first-class) — AI agents.**

- Claude Code, Codex, future Gemini sessions debugging or extending the portal.
- Consume the portal's HTTP API directly (catalog read endpoints, share-token lifecycle, admin operations) via a documented runbook served by the portal itself at `/agent-runbook`.
- The runbook + OpenAPI spec are deliberately treated as a first-class product surface, not an afterthought — see Initiative 2 in the PRD (Agent Runbook + Legacy SoT Triage).

---

## What it does (capability inventory, v1 shipped)

### Catalog browse and search

- List view + grid view of models, with thumbnails (rendered server-side per Worker section below).
- Free-text search across model name, tags, categories.
- Filter by tags, categories.
- Detail page per model: photos, renders, STL files (with selectable subset for render), notes, prints log, external links.
- 3D STL viewer in-browser via three.js + react-three-fiber (drei helpers, theme-aware materials).

### Admin operations (JWT-gated)

- Edit model metadata: name, description, tags, categories.
- Manage model files: upload, delete, mark selected-for-render subset, reorder via position field.
- Add prints to prints log (date, filament, notes, photos).
- Manage external links (URLs to MakerWorld, Printables, etc., indexed for de-duplication via migration `0011_index_ext_link_url`).
- Audit log — every admin write is recorded with user_uuid + timestamp + action + entity (see migration `0005_user_uuid_audit_log`).

### Share-link lifecycle

- Generate a share token for one model, with TTL.
- Token stored in Redis (native TTL, auto-expires).
- Anonymous access via `/share/<token>` URL — nginx-180 has explicit bypass rules for `/share/*` and `/api/share/*` so household basic-auth doesn't prompt.
- Admin can list and revoke active tokens.

### Render pipeline (async)

- Trigger via admin API: `POST /api/admin/models/<uuid>/render` (optionally pinning a subset of STL IDs).
- API enqueues an arq job in Redis.
- Worker container (separate Docker service) consumes the job: opens STL with `trimesh`, renders 4 isometric views via `matplotlib`, writes PNGs back to the `portal-content` volume as `ModelFile` rows.
- Bulk-enqueue via `infra/scripts/render-all.sh` for fresh content-volume deploys.

### Source-of-truth (SoT) ingestion

- Agent role: `POST /api/sot/models` and related write endpoints, authenticated via cookie + password flow (NOT bearer token — see project-context.md auth notes).
- Bootstrap path: `scripts/hydrate_local_tree.py` reverse-syncs `.190` portal-content back to WSL for local editing — agent token narrative replaced with cookie+password flow per 2026-05-10 correction.
- Legacy Windows/Nextcloud catalog folder under `/mnt/c/Users/ezope/Nextcloud/3d_modelowanie/` is the upstream — see its own `AGENTS.md` (cross-repo reference).

### Authentication and authorization

- JWT login via `/api/auth/login` — bcrypt password hashing.
- Refresh tokens with rotation (migration `0009_refresh_tokens`).
- CSRF middleware requires `X-Portal-Client: web` header on browser writes.
- Frontend `api()` helper (`apps/web/src/lib/api.ts`) attaches the header automatically and silently refreshes on `401 access_expired`.
- Auth dependencies pre-baked: `current_user`, `current_admin` from `app.core.auth.dependencies` — handlers receive them as `Annotated[User, Depends(...)]` parameters.
- Role enum: `admin`, `member`, `agent` (member is a v2 slot — not yet wired to per-user views).

### Observability (Initiative 1 — shipped 2026-05-10)

- Frontend errors → GlitchTip (homelab `:8800`) with debug-ID symbolication via `@sentry/vite-plugin` 5.2.x.
- Backend errors → GlitchTip via Sentry SDK.
- Structured JSON logs (api + worker) per `~/repos/configs/docs/observability-logging-contract.md`.
- OpenTelemetry distro 0.50b0 — FastAPI / Redis / SQLAlchemy instrumentation, OTLP-HTTP exporter to homelab collector.
- `infra/scripts/verify-symbolication.sh` — post-deploy smoke check that a deterministic frontend error symbolicates to a real `.tsx` source line.
- `infra/scripts/glitchtip-triage.sh` — converts a GlitchTip issue ID into a markdown bug stub for `bmad-quick-dev` or `bmad-create-story`.

### Agent-readable surface (Initiative 2 — in progress)

- `/agent-runbook` — Markdown runbook served by the portal itself (FastAPI route under `apps/api/app/modules/runbook/`), describes the URL → portal model creation flow.
- `/openapi.json` — auto-generated OpenAPI spec, enriched with operation IDs and descriptions for agent consumption.

---

## How it is deployed

### Topology

```
[Browser] ─https─▶ [nginx-180 (edge, .180)] ─proxy─▶ [.190 docker-compose stack]
                          │                                  │
                          │                                  ├─ web    (nginx + React build, 127.0.0.1:8080)
                          │                                  ├─ api    (FastAPI on uvicorn, :8000)
                          │                                  ├─ worker (arq, no HTTP port)
                          │                                  └─ redis  (queue + share tokens)
                          │
                   household basic-auth on edge
                   (bypass: /share/*, /api/share/*)

Volumes on .190:
  portal-content (rw, large)  — STL, photos, renders
  portal-state   (rw, small)  — portal.db (SQLite, ~MB)
```

### Hosts

- **`.180`** — nginx edge proxy (LXC). DDNS termination, TLS via Caddy/Let's Encrypt upstream, basic-auth gate, location-rule bypass for `/share/*`.
- **`.190`** — Docker host for the portal stack. Currently treated as **dev** for the BMAD workflow (every change deploys here automatically via `infra/scripts/deploy.sh`).
- **GlitchTip on `.190:8800`** — observability target (Initiative 1).
- **OTel collector on `.190`** — log/metric/trace sink, fans out to OpenSearch + Tempo + Prometheus elsewhere in the homelab.
- (Future `.14` or similar prod host is documented as a slot but not yet split out — `.190` is both dev and prod today.)

### Deploy ritual

```bash
bash infra/scripts/deploy.sh
```

- Builds images locally (BuildKit, with `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` secret-mounted for the vite-plugin upload step).
- Ships images to `.190` via SSH.
- Runs `docker compose up -d` to restart the stack.
- Runs Alembic migrations to bring the DB schema in sync.
- After deploy: `verify-symbolication.sh` is a non-fatal warning step (project-context.md rule — "deploy doesn't block on it but stdout is loud").

### Recovery / failure modes

- Sourcemap upload fails (issue #299 `artifactbundle/assemble` 404) → manual fallback `bash infra/scripts/upload-sourcemaps.sh` (legacy CLI flow, kept as documented recovery for one release cycle).
- SQLite DB corruption → restore from nightly backup at `/mnt/raid/3d-portal-state/backups/portal-YYYY-MM-DD.db`, 30-day retention.
- Render stuck → arq job status TTL is 1h, self-clears; restart worker container if needed.
- Share link 401 → reload nginx-180 config from `~/repos/configs/nginx/3d-portal.conf` via `bash sync.sh` in the configs repo.

---

## Architecture at a glance

Detail in [`docs/architecture.md`](architecture.md). Spec in [`docs/design/2026-04-29-portal-design.md`](design/2026-04-29-portal-design.md). Implementation plan in [`docs/plans/2026-04-29-portal-v1-implementation.md`](plans/2026-04-29-portal-v1-implementation.md).

### Repository layout (monorepo, four parts)

| Part | Path | Project type | Tech stack | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **web** | `apps/web/` | web | React 19 + Vite 6 + TS 5.6 + TanStack Router/Query + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui + three.js | v1 shipped (catalog module live; queue/printer/spools/requests slots scaffolded) |
| **api** | `apps/api/` | backend | Python 3.12 + FastAPI + SQLModel + Alembic | v1 shipped (admin/auth/runbook/share/sot modules live; queue slot reserved) |
| **worker** | `workers/render/` | backend | Python 3.12 + arq + trimesh + matplotlib + Pillow | v1 shipped |
| **infra** | `infra/` | infra | Docker Compose + nginx + bash scripts | v1 shipped |

### Data plane

- **SQLite** at `/data/state/portal.db` on the `portal-state` volume — catalog source of truth (Model, ModelFile, Tag, Category, Print, ExternalLink, User, AuditLog).
- **Filesystem** under `portal-content` — STL, photos, rendered PNGs. Referenced by `ModelFile.storage_path`.
- **Redis** — arq job queue + share-token TTL store.
- **Postgres-ready** — SQLModel + Alembic make the swap a `DATABASE_URL` flip. 11 migrations to date, all SQLite-compatible patterns.

### Web → API integration

- TanStack Query wraps `api(...)` calls in `useQuery` / `useMutation`.
- All network calls go through `api()` from `@/lib/api` — auto-attaches `X-Portal-Client: web` CSRF header, sets `credentials: "include"`, retries once on `401 access_expired` via silent refresh.
- Query-key convention: `["<bounded-context>", "<entity>", ...filters]` (e.g. `["sot", "tags", q]`).

### API → Worker integration

- arq broker = Redis. API enqueues jobs; worker consumes asynchronously.
- Worker depends on `portal-api` editable (shared SQLModel entities live in `app.core.db.models`).
- No direct HTTP between API and worker — Redis is the only contract.

---

## Future-proofing slots (already scaffolded, not yet wired)

These are partially in place — the routes/folders exist, but the implementation is empty or stub-level. Listing them so they don't show up later as "surprise" features.

| Feature | What exists today | What's missing |
|---|---|---|
| Print queue | `apps/api/app/modules/queue/` (web slot in routes/queue/), arq broker, Redis | Backend module not scaffolded; web route is placeholder |
| Moonraker integration | `apps/web/src/modules/printer/` route slot, `MOONRAKER_URL` env var, OTel collector | No backend, no client |
| Spoolman integration | `apps/web/src/modules/spools/` route slot, HTTP client target documented | No backend module |
| Print requests | `apps/web/src/modules/requests/` route slot, `User.role=member` in schema | No backend, no UI |
| Mobile photo upload | `POST /api/admin/models/{id}/prints` exists | Upload volume + reverse-rsync flow not implemented |
| OIDC / SSO | Auth isolated in `app.core.auth/` | Plug-in point exists, no client wired |
| Full-text search backend | OpenSearch on homelab (`https://192.168.2.190:9200`) | Catalog still queries SQLite directly |

---

## What is NOT in scope (deliberate exclusions)

- **Multi-tenant.** Single household, single SoT, no concept of "Michał's catalog vs. your catalog".
- **Public marketplace.** Share links are point-to-point; there's no discovery surface, no model store, no "users discover other users' models".
- **Print farm.** No queue management for multiple printers, no automated slicing, no print success/failure heuristics. The `printer/` slot is a future bridge to Moonraker (single printer), not a fleet manager.
- **Mobile-native app.** The web frontend is responsive (mobile screenshots tested) but there's no React Native / Capacitor app.
- **External authentication.** No OAuth, no SSO, no per-user accounts beyond the admin + the placeholder `member` role. Household basic-auth is the only "user model" outside admin.

## How downstream BMAD workflows should use this document

This file is the **fact-base** for backfilling the Initiative 0 / Product Foundation that was missing from the PRD. The expected chain:

1. **`bmad-product-brief`** — write a fresh `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/product-brief-3d-portal.md` that treats this overview + `architecture.md` + the existing implementation plan as inputs, and produces a proper product brief for the portal (not the GlitchTip delta).
2. **`bmad-edit-prd`** — add Initiative 0 / Product Foundation as the first H2 in `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md`, sourced from the new brief.
3. **Manual edit** — add E0 retrospective foundation to `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md` (no `bmad-edit-epics` skill ships in BMAD as of 2026-05-15).

After that backfill, the PRD will read as: Initiative 0 (this product, retroactively documented) → Initiative 1 (GlitchTip delta, shipped) → Initiative 2 (Agent Runbook + SoT triage, in-progress) → Initiative 3 (UI theme hardening, in-progress) → future initiatives appended in the same living-doc style.
